records.title: Rental deposit and repayment
records.description: A landlord may request a deposit within the statutory limit and may deduct only defined unpaid amounts or tenant-caused damage, with written specification and repayment deadlines after the tenancy.

official_source.title: Ik wil een woning huren. Mag mijn verhuurder een waarborgsom vragen?

records.ai_summary: Pay the deposit traceably, keep the initial condition report and document the final inspection. If money is withheld, ask for the written itemisation and compare it with the permitted deductions and deadlines.

records.title: Who pays for rental-home repairs
records.description: The landlord is generally responsible for major maintenance and replacement caused by normal wear, while the tenant handles minor daily repairs and damage for which the tenant is responsible.

official_source.title: Welke kosten zijn voor de huurder en welke voor de verhuurder?

records.ai_summary: Report defects promptly in writing and allow a reasonable repair opportunity. Use the official allocation table to distinguish small tenant tasks from the landlord's maintenance duty before arranging work independently.

records.title: Temporary rent reduction for serious defects
records.description: A tenant may seek temporary rent reduction when serious maintenance defects remain unresolved after written notice and the applicable waiting period, subject to the home's sector and procedure.

official_source.title: Tijdelijke huurverlaging bij ernstige gebreken vragen

records.ai_summary: Document each defect, notify the landlord in writing and preserve photographs and correspondence. The Huurcommissie route and whether its outcome is binding depend on the rental sector and contract date.

records.title: Assess and object to an annual rent increase
records.description: A rent-increase proposal must follow the rules for the rental sector, timing, frequency and maximum rent. A tenant can object through the prescribed route when the proposal is incorrect.
records.ai_summary: Check the letter, effective date, previous increase, contract and maximum rent before objecting. Send a reasoned objection to the landlord before the official deadline and retain delivery evidence.

records.title: When a landlord may end a tenancy
records.description: A landlord needs a legally recognised ground and must follow the applicable notice process. If a protected tenant does not agree, the tenancy normally continues until a court decides.

official_source.title: Mag mijn verhuurder de huur opzeggen van mijn woning?

records.ai_summary: Do not leave solely because the landlord sends a notice. Check the stated legal ground, contract type and notice term, respond in writing and obtain legal help when disputing termination.

records.title: How a tenant ends the tenancy
records.description: A tenant should give notice using a method that proves receipt, respect the notice period in the agreement and arrange inspection, meter readings, key return and a final cost settlement.

official_source.title: Hoe zeg ik de huur op van mijn woning?

records.ai_summary: Send notice early enough and obtain confirmation that the landlord received it. Agree how the home must be delivered, record the final condition and meters, and keep proof of keys and address changes.

records.title: Challenge an annual service-cost statement
records.description: A landlord's annual statement should show the actual service costs and allocation. When tenant and landlord cannot resolve a disputed calculation, the Huurcommissie may assess it under the applicable rules.
records.ai_summary: Compare the annual statement with the agreed services, advances, invoices and allocation method. First request correction and supporting documents from the landlord, then use the procedure available for the sector and contract date.

records.title: Request rent reduction based on points
records.description: A tenant whose basic rent exceeds the maximum associated with the home's official points may propose a lower rent and, when eligible, ask the Huurcommissie to assess the amount.
records.ai_summary: Run the official rent check, send the landlord a documented proposal with the calculated points and observe the required effective-date timing. Eligibility differs by sector and contract circumstances.

records.title: Starting a Huurcommissie procedure
records.description: Huurcommissie cases cover defined rent, increase, service-cost and defect disputes. The applicant must select the correct procedure, provide evidence and may owe a case fee depending on the outcome.
records.ai_summary: Try the required landlord step first, choose the matching case type and submit the contract, letters, calculations and other evidence. Read the outcome and challenge route carefully because legal effect varies by case.

records.title: Report unfair landlord practices to the municipality
records.description: Every municipality provides a reporting point for unwanted landlord practices such as intimidation, discrimination, excessive deposits, unreasonable charges or missing required information.

official_source.title: Wat betekent de Wet goed verhuurderschap voor huurders en woningzoekenden?

records.ai_summary: Preserve the contract, payment records and messages, then report the conduct to the municipality where the home is located. Municipal enforcement and recovery of private money are different routes and may require separate action.
